subject: UX & Research
title: Richard Saul Wurman and IA
description: A quick win: understand Richard Saul Wurman and IA.

hook.text: Richard Saul Wurman coined 'information architecture' in 1976. He also founded the TED conference.

buildup.text: Wurman saw that the information explosion was creating anxiety. People drowned in data but couldn't find knowledge.

discovery.text: He proposed LATCH: five ways to organize anything — Location, Alphabet, Time, Category, and Hierarchy.

twist.text: LATCH covers every information structure you'll ever need. There are no other fundamental organizing principles.

climax.text: Every menu, timeline, and sorted list uses LATCH. It's the most universal framework in information design.

punchline.text: Five ways to organize anything: location, alphabet, time, category, hierarchy.

quiz.question: What does LATCH stand for in information architecture?
